key_points: Rawls dorms described as ugly with exposed sinks mid-room
Smith Hall has reported mold; LLC WiFi is notably bad
South Hall is isolated, cheapest, and hard to find info on
key_quotes: it's so ugly and it decreases my motivation to even wanna go to college. imagine u have to go back to sleep in a literally teachers closet
Smith has mold, and Rawls has fucked up ac/heat
I wouldn't be caught dead in a ratass UM dorm

key_points: Heavily commuter campus — weekends are dead on campus
Parties exist but you pay to get in, per student reports
Greek life present but small; fraternities called out for hazing
key_quotes: Since the University of Memphis is a commuter campus there wont be much to do on weekends
there like no party's that are here and that weird that you have to pay to get into one
Absolutely do not join Kappa Sigma... They are all scummy assholes who haze and protect rapist

key_points: Meal plan widely considered not worth the cost
Tiger Den food quality called 'rough'; hours are inconvenient
Mandatory $300 TigerBucks fee even if you skip the meal plan
key_quotes: Meal plan not worth it in my opinion, either. Hours are wonky, quality is meh for the price
The $300 tigerbucks is NOT optional, its a jobs program basically
As far as I know it's only required for freshman who live on campus. Your mileage may vary but it's not a great plan

key_points: University hosts suicide prevention walks — awareness is present
Free counseling sessions advertised through campus groups
No specific complaints about counseling wait times in data
key_quotes: Our group is offering a free counseling session with an optional paid research component
I graduated high school depressed and unmotivated... I never thought I'd be able to accomplish this

key_points: Refund delays are a recurring, consistent complaint from students
Summer aid is significantly reduced — expect out-of-pocket costs
Merit scholarships exist but community service requirements are confusing
key_quotes: Mine still hasn't zeroed out. I've been at the UM for six years and this has never happened
Summer FAFSA is usually not a lot of money, it's around 50% of your normal aid for a semester
I advise emailing the scholarship office... that seems like more service hours than some of the other merit scholarships

key_points: Some professors have serious language barrier issues in STEM classes
Honors College praised for early registration and exclusive funding
Global Career Accelerator program called overpriced busy work
key_quotes: Help! How can I learn this math/Calculus when I cannot understand 90% of the instructor's English??
Getting to sign up for courses before anyone else was absolutely worth it
Don't waste your money on it... Double tuition and a program fee of $1600 it was not worth it

key_points: Administrative staff repeatedly described as incompetent and unresponsive
Student slept in car after admin failed to allow after-hours check-in
Zero communication reported from graduate program admissions offices
key_quotes: it seems like the administrative officers barely passed with a GED
I had to sleep in my loaded car because a student worker wouldn't let me check in after hours, which was said to be allowed
I was so excited to get accepted but the only thing I got so far was an acceptance letter, nobody reaching out to me before and after

key_points: Campus itself considered safe; Memphis city crime is real but localized
Campus described as beautiful by multiple prospective students
Parking is a consistent headache — took forever to find spots
key_quotes: Memphis has a high crime rate in impoverished areas of the city. Stay away from bad areas and you will be fine
The University of Memphis has a safe campus... The campus is the safest campus in TN
parking took forever to find lol

key_points: No strong alumni network or recruiting pipeline mentioned by students
Global Career Accelerator program seen as a waste of money
R1 research status noted but comp sci research culture unclear to students
key_quotes: Don't waste your money on it or if you do don't choose the career rotation option as it waters down the experience
internships and work experience looks better on a resume than these [honor societies]
I am going to apply for fall 26 for the comp sci program at UoM. My COA would be around 20k... is it really research oriented since it's a R1 institution

key_points: In-state affordability is the primary draw for most students
Out-of-state or $60k in debt scenarios questioned seriously
International students get no scholarships and face budget problems
key_quotes: The University of Memphis would leave me in about $60,000 in debt after 4 years
I applyed to University of Memphis and got accepted... but I got no scholarships... My family maximum budget is around 30k a year so I won't be able to attend
don't worry, they let in anybody with a pulse. you got this!

key_points: Diverse, welcoming campus culture praised by LGBTQ+ and international students
Strong commuter energy — social life requires active effort to find
Students seem scrappy and practical, not prestige-chasing
key_quotes: We have a really diverse campus. That is part of the reason why I came here
I love it, but this whole COVID thing has made it a little different
UofM is great! The heat will get you before crime, fr fr

key_points: **Administration:** Students left without housing help after family deaths, sleeping in cars
**Greek Life:** Fraternities explicitly accused of hazing and protecting rapists
**DEI:** Multicultural Affairs Office shut down with no mitigation plan from leadership
key_quotes: They are all scummy assholes who haze and protect rapist. Every single one of them
It was handled badly. There was no mitigation on UofM leadership's part. They knew this was coming down the pipeline
I had to sleep in my loaded car because a student worker wouldn't let me check in after hours, which was said to be allowed

key_points: On-campus medical clinic is cheap, highly rated, and student-friendly
Honors College offers Japan study abroad, conference funding, and early registration
Stonewall Tigers LGBTQ+ org is active and genuinely inclusive
key_quotes: I've been there! The staff is great and my most expensive appointment was $25
There are a lot of opportunities if you want to take advantage of them...honors only study abroad trips (this year is Japan)...funding to present research at conferences
Not black or trans, but have attended multiple events by the student group Stonewall Tigers and they are a great organization and have a pretty diverse student population
